content.text: Subscription vending is the automated process of provisioning a new Azure subscription fully configured and governance-ready — with management group placement, RBAC, budget alerts, network connectivity, required tags, and diagnostic settings applied — before the requesting team receives access. The term "vending" deliberately mirrors the instant, consistent delivery of a vending machine: the requestor gets a ready-to-use subscription, not a blank canvas that must be manually configured by the cloud team.

Without subscription vending, cloud teams manually provision subscriptions, a process that is slow (days to weeks), error-prone, and inconsistent. Different team members apply different policies, network configurations, and RBAC assignments. Vending replaces this with a pipeline that runs the same configuration every time, in minutes.

attrs.alt: Subscription vending pipeline: Request → Approve → Provision → Configure → Validate

content.text: The Five Stages of a Subscription Vending Pipeline

The requesting team submits a structured request describing what they need. Common intake mechanisms include a self-service portal (a web form backed by an API), a Git pull request that adds a YAML or JSON file describing the new subscription, or a ServiceNow/Jira catalog item that triggers a webhook.

The request should capture the minimum information needed for automation: workload name, environment tier, owning team, cost centre, required regions, and whether the workload needs network connectivity (spoke VNet peered to the hub).

# subscription-request.yaml — submitted as a PR to the subscriptions-catalog repo
apiVersion: subscriptions.platform/v1
kind: SubscriptionRequest
metadata:
  name: payments-production
spec:
  displayName: prod-payments
  strategyTier: corp-prod          # corp-prod | corp-nonprod | online | sandbox
  workload: payments
  environment: production
  ownerTeamGroupId: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
  costCenter: CC-1001
  monthlyBudgetGbp: 5000
  requiresNetworkConnectivity: true
  allowedRegions:
    - eastus
    - eastus2

The approval stage ensures the cloud team validates the request before provisioning. In a Git-based workflow this is a pull request review — a cloud platform engineer must approve and merge the PR. In a portal-based workflow it is a workflow approval task. The approval gate prevents sandbox subscriptions from requesting corp-prod placement, catches invalid cost centres, and gives the platform team visibility into incoming workloads.

Once approved, the pipeline creates the Azure subscription via the billing API (for EA) or the Subscription API (for MCA) and immediately places it in the correct management group. This step runs within minutes of approval completion and requires a service principal with billing account administrator permissions.

Configuration runs immediately after placement. The pipeline applies a consistent baseline: RBAC assignments for the requesting team (Owner or Contributor at subscription scope), a monthly budget with alert email, diagnostic settings sending the subscription Activity Log to the central Log Analytics workspace, required tags (environment, workload, cost-center), and — for Corp subscriptions — a spoke VNet peered to the regional hub.

After configuration, the pipeline runs an Azure Policy compliance check and confirms all baseline policies are satisfied. It then sends the requesting team an email or Slack message with the subscription ID, resource group name conventions, and links to the cost dashboard. If any configuration step failed, the platform team is alerted instead.

The following Azure DevOps pipeline implements a full subscription vending workflow triggered on a PR merge to the subscriptions-catalog repository. It reads the YAML request file, provisions the subscription, places it in the correct management group, and applies baseline configuration.

# azure-pipelines-vend.yml
trigger:
  branches:
    include:
      - main
  paths:
    include:
      - requests/*.yaml

pool:
  vmImage: ubuntu-latest

variables:
  BILLING_SCOPE: /providers/Microsoft.Billing/billingAccounts/$(BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID)/enrollmentAccounts/$(ENROLLMENT_ACCOUNT_ID)

steps:
  - task: AzureCLI@2
    displayName: Parse and validate subscription request
    inputs:
      azureSubscription: governance-service-connection
      scriptType: bash
      scriptLocation: inlineScript
      inlineScript: |
        # Find the changed request file
        CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD -- requests/*.yaml | head -1)
        echo "Processing: $CHANGED"

        # Parse the request YAML using yq
        DISPLAY_NAME=$(yq '.spec.displayName' $CHANGED)
        TIER=$(yq '.spec.strategyTier' $CHANGED)
        OWNER_OID=$(yq '.spec.ownerTeamGroupId' $CHANGED)
        COST_CENTER=$(yq '.spec.costCenter' $CHANGED)
        BUDGET=$(yq '.spec.monthlyBudgetGbp' $CHANGED)
        NETWORK=$(yq '.spec.requiresNetworkConnectivity' $CHANGED)

        # Map tier to management group
        case $TIER in
          corp-prod)    MG=mg-corp-prod ;;
          corp-nonprod) MG=mg-corp-nonprod ;;
          online)       MG=mg-online ;;
          sandbox)      MG=mg-sandbox ;;
          *) echo 'ERROR: unknown tier'; exit 1 ;;
        esac

        # Write parsed values to pipeline variables for subsequent steps
        echo "##vso[task.setvariable variable=DISPLAY_NAME]$DISPLAY_NAME"
        echo "##vso[task.setvariable variable=TARGET_MG]$MG"
        echo "##vso[task.setvariable variable=OWNER_OID]$OWNER_OID"
        echo "##vso[task.setvariable variable=COST_CENTER]$COST_CENTER"
        echo "##vso[task.setvariable variable=BUDGET]$BUDGET"
        echo "##vso[task.setvariable variable=NEEDS_NETWORK]$NETWORK"

  - task: AzureCLI@2
    displayName: Create subscription
    inputs:
      azureSubscription: governance-service-connection
      scriptType: bash
      scriptLocation: inlineScript
      inlineScript: |
        # Create subscription via EA enrollment account
        RESULT=$(az account create \
          --display-name "$(DISPLAY_NAME)" \
          --enrollment-account-object-id "${ENROLLMENT_ACCOUNT_ID}" \
          --billing-account-name "${BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}" \
          --offer-type MS-AZR-0017P \
          -o json)

        SUB_ID=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.subscriptionId')
        echo "Created subscription: $SUB_ID"
        echo "##vso[task.setvariable variable=SUB_ID]$SUB_ID"

  - task: AzureCLI@2
    displayName: Place in management group
    inputs:
      azureSubscription: governance-service-connection
      scriptType: bash
      scriptLocation: inlineScript
      inlineScript: |
        az account management-group subscription add \
          --name "$(TARGET_MG)" \
          --subscription "$(SUB_ID)"
        echo "Placed $(SUB_ID) in $(TARGET_MG)"

  - task: AzureCLI@2
    displayName: Apply baseline configuration
    inputs:
      azureSubscription: governance-service-connection
      scriptType: bash
      scriptLocation: inlineScript
      inlineScript: |
        SUB_SCOPE="/subscriptions/$(SUB_ID)"

        # RBAC: Owner for the requesting team
        az role assignment create \
          --assignee-object-id "$(OWNER_OID)" \
          --role Owner \
          --scope $SUB_SCOPE

        # Tags
        az tag create --resource-id $SUB_SCOPE \
          --tags cost-center=$(COST_CENTER) managed-by=platform

        # Budget
        az consumption budget create \
          --budget-name monthly-budget \
          --amount $(BUDGET) \
          --time-grain Monthly \
          --start-date $(date -d 'first day of this month' +%Y-%m-%d) \
          --end-date $(date -d 'first day of this month + 1 year' +%Y-%m-%d) \
          --scope $SUB_SCOPE \
          --notifications '[{"enabled":true,"operator":"GreaterThan","threshold":80,"contactEmails":["platform@example.com"]}]'

  - task: AzureCLI@2
    displayName: Compliance check
    inputs:
      azureSubscription: governance-service-connection
      scriptType: bash
      scriptLocation: inlineScript
      inlineScript: |
        echo 'Waiting 90s for policy evaluation...'
        sleep 90
        az policy state summarize \
          --subscription "$(SUB_ID)" \
          --query '{Compliant:results.policyStates.compliantCount,NonCompliant:results.policyStates.noncompliantCount}' \
          -o json

The pipeline above requires a service connection with billing administrator permissions for subscription creation and management group administrator for placement. Separate these from the service connection used for workload deployments — vending requires higher privilege than day-to-day deployments.

For teams using GitOps with Terraform, subscription vending can be entirely Terraform-driven. The requesting team opens a PR that adds an entry to a `subscriptions.auto.tfvars.json` file; CI runs `terraform plan` for review, and merging triggers `terraform apply`. This approach uses the existing Terraform state as the source of truth for all provisioned subscriptions.

For Corp subscriptions that need network connectivity, the vending pipeline must also create the spoke VNet and peer it to the hub. This is the most workload-specific configuration step — the spoke address space must not overlap with any existing VNet in the hub or other spokes.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# network-bootstrap.sh — called by the vending pipeline for corp-prod subs
# Assumes the SPOKE_PREFIX variable is set by the caller (e.g., 10.1.4.0/24)

SUB_ID=$1
SPOKE_PREFIX=$2
HUB_VNET_ID=$3   # resource ID of the hub VNet
HUB_SUB=$4

# Create a resource group for the spoke networking components
az group create \
  --name rg-network-spoke \
  --location eastus \
  --subscription $SUB_ID

# Create the spoke VNet
SPOKE_VNET_ID=$(az network vnet create \
  --name vnet-spoke \
  --resource-group rg-network-spoke \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --address-prefixes $SPOKE_PREFIX \
  --query 'newVNet.id' -o tsv)

# Peer spoke → hub (from spoke subscription)
az network vnet peering create \
  --name spoke-to-hub \
  --resource-group rg-network-spoke \
  --vnet-name vnet-spoke \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --remote-vnet $HUB_VNET_ID \
  --allow-vnet-access true \
  --allow-forwarded-traffic true

# Peer hub → spoke (from hub subscription — requires Hub Owner RBAC)
az network vnet peering create \
  --name hub-to-spoke-${SUB_ID:0:8} \
  --resource-group rg-network-hub \
  --vnet-name vnet-hub \
  --subscription $HUB_SUB \
  --remote-vnet $SPOKE_VNET_ID \
  --allow-vnet-access true \
  --allow-gateway-transit true

echo "Spoke VNet $SPOKE_VNET_ID peered to hub $HUB_VNET_ID"

Address space management is the hardest operational problem in hub-and-spoke vending. Allocate from a central IPAM (IP Address Management) tool — Azure VirtualNetwork Manager or a third-party IPAM — rather than assigning prefixes manually. Overlapping spoke prefixes cause silent routing failures that are difficult to diagnose.

The vending pipeline should be idempotent: running it again for an already-provisioned subscription must not create duplicate resources or error. Use `--if-not-exists` flags where available, and check before creating in bash. Terraform handles this automatically through state.

# Idempotent role assignment — skip if already exists
EXISTING=$(az role assignment list \
  --assignee $OWNER_OID \
  --role Owner \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID \
  --query 'length(@)' -o tsv)

if [ "$EXISTING" -eq 0 ]; then
  az role assignment create \
    --assignee-object-id $OWNER_OID \
    --role Owner \
    --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID
  echo 'Owner assignment created'
else
  echo 'Owner assignment already exists, skipping'
fi

# Idempotent budget creation — delete and recreate is safe for budgets
az consumption budget delete \
  --budget-name monthly-budget \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID 2>/dev/null || true

az consumption budget create \
  --budget-name monthly-budget \
  --amount $BUDGET \
  --time-grain Monthly \
  --start-date $(date -d 'first day of this month' +%Y-%m-%d) \
  --end-date $(date -d 'first day of this month + 1 year' +%Y-%m-%d) \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID \
  --notifications '[{"enabled":true,"operator":"GreaterThan","threshold":80,"contactEmails":["platform@example.com"]}]'
Creating the subscription but not immediately placing it in a MG
Subscription lands under Root with no policies applied; workloads deploy without guardrails
Make MG placement the very next step after creation; treat placement as part of provisioning, not configuration
Not pre-validating spoke address space for conflicts
VNet peering fails silently; network team manually cleans up overlapping prefixes
Query Azure VNet Manager or IPAM before the pipeline runs; fail fast with a clear error
Granting Owner to an individual user instead of a group
When the user leaves, no one has access; offboarding creates orphaned subscriptions
Always assign Owner to an Entra ID group, never to an individual user principal
Not setting a budget on vended subscriptions
Runaway costs in sandbox or test subscriptions go undetected until the invoice arrives
Budget creation is a non-optional vending step; fail the pipeline if it does not succeed
Non-compliant resources deployed before the team realises inherited policies block them
Wait 60–120s after placement, then poll az policy state summarize before sending the ready notification
Using the same service principal for vending and workload deployments
Blast radius: a compromised workload identity can create new subscriptions
Separate the vending service principal (billing admin) from workload deployment identities; vault the vending credentials separately

Subscription vending replaces ad-hoc manual provisioning with a repeatable, automated pipeline that delivers a fully configured, governance-ready subscription in minutes. The five stages — request, approve, provision, configure, validate — map onto either a portal workflow or a GitOps pull request process. Every vended subscription is placed in the correct management group, inherits the right policies, has RBAC assigned to a group (not an individual), carries a budget alert, and — for Corp subscriptions — has a peered spoke VNet. Treating vending as a product rather than a manual process is the foundation of a scalable Azure platform.
